description: Autocomplete filtering uses String.prototype.includes() (substring matching) instead of prefix matching as specified by REQ-TAG-005. The spec states 'filtered by user-typed prefix, case-insensitive' (behavioral-spec.md line 78). The implementation at FeedRow.svelte:74 uses `e.name.includes(prefix)` which matches substrings anywhere in the tag name — e.g., typing 'es' would match 'rest' even though 'es' is not a prefix of 'rest'. Correct implementation should use `e.name.startsWith(prefix)`.
expected: `e.name.startsWith(prefix)` — only tags whose names begin with the typed prefix should appear in suggestions.
actual: `e.name.includes(prefix)` — any tag containing the typed text as a substring anywhere in its name appears in suggestions.
recommendation: Change line 74 from `.filter((e) => e.name.includes(prefix))` to `.filter((e) => e.name.startsWith(prefix))`. Alternatively, if substring matching is intentionally desired, update REQ-TAG-005 line 78 to say 'filtered by user-typed substring' instead of 'prefix'. Add a test case that distinguishes behavior: with tags ['react', 'rest', 'forest'], typing 'es' should not match 'forest' with prefix matching, but would with substring matching. Note: this is a pre-existing issue (filtering logic implemented in sprint 1-2), not introduced by sprint 3. Route to Phase 2b for implementation fix or Phase 1a for spec clarification.

specReference.snippet: **State-driven**: WHILE the tag input is focused, THE SYSTEM SHALL show a dropdown list of matching `TagInventory.entries` (filtered by user-typed prefix, case-insensitive), ordered by `usageCount` descending.
